Succeeded invocation is only an audit precondition for later validation/artifact/decision flow. SPLIT_STRATEGY_INVOCATION_RECORDED stores only invocation summary, result_action/result_digest, error kind, and short error summary, not the full SplitStrategyResult body; full DecompositionProposal and MergePlan bodies are persisted by later artifact events. MergePlan is clarified as a plugin-generated instance contract for one expansion that references plugin MergePolicy/merge_policy_id; it is not a protocol-defined merge rule or algorithm. The same expansion_scope may have multiple split strategy invocation audit attempts using split_invocation:{expansion_scope_hash}:attempt:{invocation_attempt_no}, but accepted EXPANSION_DECISION_RECORDED is unique per expansion_scope via expansion_decision:{expansion_scope_hash}. Proposal and merge plan keys include proposal_digest / merge_plan_digest only to dedupe identical artifacts; different digests in the same scope are duplicate/conflict, not competing valid plans. TASK_EXPANDED is derived from accepted expansion_decision_id and child unit/relation IDs are deterministic. Feature list JSON parse passed; powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\init.ps1 passed with pytest collected 30 items and 30 passed in 0.67s.
